bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: 4Q26 adjusted EPS exceeded both Goldman Sachs' estimate and consensus.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: BBY reported 4Q26 adj. EPS of $2.61, above the GS estimate of $2.50 and consensus (Factset) of $2.46.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Adjusted EBIT margin increased year-over-year and surpassed estimates.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Domestic gross margin increased year-over-year and was above Goldman Sachs' estimate.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Domestic gross margin increased 3 bps y/y to 20.9%, above the GS estimate of 20.8%.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Growth in Best Buy Ads and Marketplace contributed positively to the gross margin rate.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Domestic adjusted SG&A expense decreased year-over-year due to lower compensation and Best Buy Health expenses.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: The adjusted SG&A expense ratio decreased year-over-year and was in-line with Goldman Sachs' estimate.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: The adj. SG&A expense ratio decreased 11 bps y/y to 15.9%, in-line with the GS estimate of 15.9%.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: The stock is trading up in pre-market, indicating a positive market reaction to the better-than-expected bottom line.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: The stock is trading up in the pre-market, likely due to the better than expected bottom line.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Goldman Sachs maintains a 'Buy' rating on BBY.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: There is a significant 51.0% upside potential to the 12-month price target.

relationships.original_sentence: Sales decreased -1.0% y/y to $13.8bn and enterprise comparable sales decreased -0.8% y/y, below the GS estimate of +1.2% and consensus of +0.1%.

relationships.relationship_description: Provides consensus estimates for BBY's enterprise comparable sales.
relationships.original_sentence: Sales decreased -1.0% y/y to $13.8bn and enterprise comparable sales decreased -0.8% y/y, below the GS estimate of +1.2% and consensus of +0.1%.

relationships.relationship_description: Provides estimates for BBY's adjusted EBIT margin.
relationships.original_sentence: Adj. EBIT margin increased 8 bps y/y to 5.0%, above GS/Cons. estimates of 4.9%/4.9%.

relationships.relationship_description: Provides consensus estimates for BBY's adjusted EBIT margin.
relationships.original_sentence: Adj. EBIT margin increased 8 bps y/y to 5.0%, above GS/Cons. estimates of 4.9%/4.9%.

relationships.relationship_description: Provides consensus estimates for BBY's FY27 revenue guidance.
relationships.original_sentence: BBY introduced FY27 guidance, including revenue of $41.2bn-$42.1bn (vs. consensus of $42.2bn), comparable sales of -1.0% to +1.0% (vs. consensus of +1.4%), adj. EBIT margin of 4.3% to 4.4% (vs. consensus 4.3%), and adj. EPS of $6.30-$6.60 (vs. consensus of $6.63).

relationships.relationship_description: Provides estimates for BBY's domestic gross margin.
relationships.original_sentence: Domestic gross margin increased 3 bps y/y to 20.9%, above the GS estimate of n 20.8%.

relationships.relationship_description: Provides estimates for BBY's adjusted SG&A expense ratio.
relationships.original_sentence: The adj. SG&A expense ratio decreased 11 bps y/y to 15.9%, in-line with the GS estimate of 15.9%.

relationships.relationship_description: BBY risks losing market share to other retailers.
relationships.original_sentence: Downside risks include if demand for consumer electronics remains pressured for longer than expected, weighing on BBY’s top line and margin; if the upgrade/replacement cycle is deferred for longer than normal, limiting BBY’s potential sales growth; if innovation fails to drive demand, or if the level of innovation is less than expected; if the broader macro environment continues to weigh on demand for higher-ticket items; if BBY loses share to other retailers, particularly online-only retailers; if industry-wide promotional levels increase, weighing on gross margin; if wage and other costs are higher than expected, driving further operating margin compression; if consumers trade down into lower-priced products and or seek value at other retailers; and if shrink increases, creating a more intense margin headwind.

relationships.relationship_description: BBY risks losing market share to online-only retailers.
relationships.original_sentence: Downside risks include if demand for consumer electronics remains pressured for longer than expected, weighing on BBY’s top line and margin; if the upgrade/replacement cycle is deferred for longer than normal, limiting BBY’s potential sales growth; if innovation fails to drive demand, or if the level of innovation is less than expected; if the broader macro environment continues to weigh on demand for higher-ticket items; if BBY loses share to other retailers, particularly online-only retailers; if industry-wide promotional levels increase, weighing on gross margin; if wage and other costs are higher than expected, driving further operating margin compression; if consumers trade down into lower-priced products and or seek value at other retailers; and if shrink increases, creating a more intense margin headwind.
